content:
1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
239

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`. Existence of such a witness is the single
17	remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`);
18	the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all project from the witness directly.
19	
20	The file contains:
21	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
22	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
23	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
24	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
25	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: the (deferred) existence of such a witness — the single
26	  Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and genus-`0` rigidity.
27	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
28	  Albanese witness.
29	
30	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
31	
32	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
33	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
34	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
35	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
36	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
37	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
38	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
39	-/
40	
41	set_option autoImplicit false
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
48	
49	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
50	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
51	  [IsProper C.hom]
52	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
53	
54	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
55	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
56	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
57	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
58	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
59	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
60	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
61	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
62	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
63	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
64	
65	namespace IsAlbanese
66	
67	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
68	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
69	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
70	  Classical.choose h
71	
72	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
73	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
74	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
75	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
76	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
77	
78	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
79	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
80	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
81	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
82	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
83	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
84	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
85	
86	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
87	universal morphisms. -/
88	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
89	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
90	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
91	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
92	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
93	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
94	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
95	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
96	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
97	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
98	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
99	    simp
100	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
101	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
102	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
103	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
104	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
105	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
106	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
107	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
108	    simp
109	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
110	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
111	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
112	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
113	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
114	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
115	
116	end IsAlbanese
117	
118	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
119	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
120	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
121	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
122	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
123	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
124	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
125	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
126	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
127	
128	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
129	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
130	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
131	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
132	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
133	
134	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
135	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
136	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
137	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
138	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
139	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
140	
141	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
142	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
143	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
144	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
145	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
146	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
147	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
148	  grpObj : GrpObj J
149	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
150	  proper : IsProper J.hom
151	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
152	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
153	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
154	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
155	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
156	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
157	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
158	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
159	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
160	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
161	
162	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
163	curve. This is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C Jacobian
164	scaffolding: it packages all five protected sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
165	`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`)
166	into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records that the Albanese property of the
167	underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every choice of $k$-rational marked point
168	`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor` field of `JacobianWitness`).
169	
170	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
171	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
172	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
173	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
174	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
175	existence is assumed and the proof is deferred to a future iteration. -/
176	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
177	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
178	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) :=
179	  sorry
180	
181	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
182	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
183	on it. -/
184	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
185	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
186	    JacobianWitness C :=
187	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
188	
189	-- data
190	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
191	
192	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
193	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
194	witness is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding.
195	The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the witness — a smooth proper
196	geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of relative dimension `0` is
197	`Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is absorbed into
198	`nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
199	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
200	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
201	  (jacobianWitness C).J
202	
203	namespace Jacobian
204	
205	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
206	
207	-- data
208	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
209	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
210	
211	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
212	genus of `C`. -/
213	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
214	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
215	
216	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
217	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
218	
219	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
220	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
221	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
222	
223	end Jacobian
224	
225	end AlgebraicGeometry
226

input.content:
# Blueprint Writer Report

## Slug
jacobian-iter118

## Status
COMPLETE

All three must-fix items from the directive (Fix 1, Fix 2, Fix 3) are
addressed; no proof-block content of `thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness` and
no protected-instance theorem block were modified.

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex

## Changes Made

- **Fix 1 — Revised** `thm:IsAlbanese_unique` (statement block, replacing
  the iter-117 "uniquely isomorphic by an isomorphism" prose). New prose
  introduces named morphisms $\iota_i := h_i.\mathtt{ofCurve}$ and states
  the conclusion as "there is a unique morphism $e \colon J_1 \to J_2$
  satisfying $\iota_2 = \iota_1 \circ e$", matching the Lean signature
  `∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e` exactly. Prose
  acknowledges that $e$ is an isomorphism but flags that the iso content
  is not exposed in the conclusion. `\uses{}` augmented with
  `def:IsAlbanese_ofCurve`.

- **Fix 1 (companion) — Added remark** `rem:IsAlbanese_unique_iso`. One-paragraph
  remark immediately after `thm:IsAlbanese_unique` documenting that the Lean
  proof internally computes the inverse morphism `h` and verifies
  `g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁`, `h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂` (Lean lines 104, 113) before returning
  only the morphism-and-uniqueness triple. Names the natural strengthening
  `∃! (e : J₁ ≅ J₂), ...` and labels this as a Lean-side refactor candidate
  out of iter-118 scope, with a note that downstream consumers in the
  protected `AbelJacobi.Jacobian.*` interface use only the morphism content.

- **Fix 2 — Added new `\subsection`** "The Albanese witness bundle" inside
  Section "Existence of an Albanese variety", placed before
  `thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`. Contains three new blocks:
  - **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:JacobianWitness}`/`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.JacobianWitness}` —
    enumerates all seven fields of the structure: `J`, `grpObj`, `proper`,
    `smooth`, `geomIrred`, `smoothGenus` (with note that it refines `smooth`),
    `isAlbaneseFor : ∀ P, IsAlbanese C P J`.
  - **Added remark** `\label{rem:JacobianWitness_quantifier_order}` — design-choice
    remark explaining the $\exists J, \forall P$ vs classical $\forall P, \exists J$
    quantifier reversal, with mathematical justification (translation by a point is
    an automorphism, so $J$ is intrinsic) and pragmatic justification (clean
    per-$P$ projection in `AbelJacobi.Jacobian.*`).
  - **Added remark** `\label{rem:JacobianWitness_smooth_redundancy}` — the optional
    one-line remark on `smooth` vs `smoothGenus` redundancy, citing Mathlib's
    `SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth` and explaining that both fields are kept
    for projection-site convenience.

- **Fix 2 (companion) — Revised** `thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness` statement block
